commonIssues:
Applicants, especially first-generation learners, often struggle to provide mandatory paternal-side proof, such as an SC/ST certificate of the father, brother, or paternal blood relative.

How to fix:

  • First apply for the father's caste certificate if he doesn't have one — apply via Delhi e-District Portal.
  • If the father is deceased, submit the death certificate along with an SC/ST certificate of a paternal uncle or brother.
  • Obtain old school records of the father mentioning caste from the respective school.
  • Submit a sworn affidavit along with the father's birth certificate or voter ID mentioning community.

Those who migrated from other states often experience extreme difficulty in getting their certificates processed, requiring extensive verification from their state of origin.

How to fix:

  • Obtain a caste certificate from your state of origin first — this is usually mandatory.
  • Submit the original state certificate along with Delhi residence proof (3+ years) to the SDM office.
  • Provide voter ID, Aadhaar, or ration card showing Delhi address as residence proof.
  • If your original state office is unresponsive, request a verification letter through the Delhi SDM office to your origin state.

Users have reported issues with the portal not accepting documents due to strict file size restrictions (e.g., 50KB limits), difficulty uploading files, and issues with verification codes.

How to fix:

  • Compress documents to under 50KB using SmallPDF or TinyPNG for images.
  • Upload in JPEG format (not PDF) to meet strict size requirements.
  • Use Google Chrome (latest version), clear cache, and disable ad-blockers.
  • If CAPTCHA/verification code fails, refresh the page and retry.
  • Alternatively, visit the nearest Common Service Centre (CSC) for assisted filing on the e-District Portal.

The system requires a live photo or a photo matching the Aadhaar card, which often leads to rejection if not matched correctly, requiring physical verification.

How to fix:

  • Use a recent passport-size photo that closely matches your Aadhaar photo.
  • If photo matching fails online, visit the Sub-Division Office (SDO) for physical verification with original Aadhaar.
  • Update your Aadhaar photo at the nearest Aadhaar Seva Kendra if it's outdated.
  • Ensure the photo is in JPEG format, white background, and under 50KB.

Applicants must provide a ration card or electricity bill, which can be challenging for those without stable housing or updated documents.

How to fix:

  • Use Aadhaar card, Voter ID, or Electricity Bill as primary residence proof.
  • If you're a tenant, submit a rent agreement along with the landlord's electricity bill or property tax receipt.
  • Apply for a Delhi Domicile Certificate via e-District Portal if needed as supplementary proof.
  • A bank passbook with Delhi address can also serve as supporting residence proof.

Applications are frequently stalled with 'Query Raised' by the department, asking for additional documents or re-submission, which requires visiting the SDO office.

How to fix:

  • Log in to the e-District Portal → Reports → Submission Status to see the specific query.
  • Upload the requested additional documents through the portal's response section.
  • If physical visit is required, go to the SDM office with all original documents during working hours.
  • If the query seems unreasonable, file a grievance at Delhi PGMS Portal.

While a 14-day SLA exists for SC certificates, local enquiries and verification of paternal documents often result in long delays, causing students to miss education or scholarship deadlines.

How to fix:

  • Track your application status on e-District Portal regularly.
  • If delayed beyond the 14-day SLA, file a grievance at Delhi PGMS Portal.
  • Escalate to the District Magistrate (DM) office or attend the Jan Sunwai (public hearing) conducted by the DM.
  • For urgent academic purposes, request an interim acknowledgement from the SDM office.

If an application is rejected, a citizen is only allowed to re-apply once, creating high stakes and potential legal hurdles.

How to fix:

  • Carefully review the rejection reason on the e-District portal before re-applying.
  • Ensure all missing/incorrect documents are addressed in the re-application.
  • If re-application is also rejected, file a Writ Petition in the Delhi High Court — consult a lawyer or DLSA for free legal aid.
  • Contact the Delhi State Legal Services Authority for free legal assistance.

Revenue officers often conduct physical inquiries at the applicant's residence, which some applicants find intrusive or intimidating.

How to fix:

  • Keep all original documents ready at home during the enquiry period.
  • Request the officer to show their official ID before allowing inspection.
  • If facing harassment or bribery demands, file a complaint at Delhi PGMS Portal.
  • You can also call the Delhi Government Anti-Corruption Helpline 1031 for reporting misconduct.

Applicants for OBC certificates face strict scrutiny regarding their income and 'creamy layer' status, which requires detailed, up-to-date documentation.

How to fix:

  • Obtain a Non-Creamy Layer (NCL) certificate separately — apply via e-District Portal.
  • Provide the latest 3-year Income Tax Returns (ITR) of both parents to prove non-creamy layer status.
  • If parents are salaried, submit salary slips; if self-employed, submit CA-certified income details.
  • The current creamy layer income limit is ₹8 lakhs per annum — check the latest DoPT notification for updates.
